branding.headline: Switch from Adobe Acrobat and Save Thousands
branding.subheadline: Tungsten Power PDF delivers everything your team needs — editing, redaction, e-signatures, and Microsoft 365 integration — at a fraction of Adobe's annual subscription cost. Perpetual licensing available. Local Australian support.

benefits.title: Pay once, own it forever
benefits.body: Power PDF Standard and Advanced are perpetual licences. Buy once, own it permanently — no annual renewal, no forced subscriptions, no surprise price increases.

benefits.title: Full PDF editing and conversion
benefits.body: Create, edit, and convert PDFs to and from Word, Excel, PowerPoint and more. Office-style interface means your team is productive from day one.

benefits.title: Redaction and compliance
benefits.body: Advanced edition includes professional redaction, Bates numbering, password protection, and digital signatures — built for legal, government, and compliance workflows.

benefits.title: Microsoft 365 and SharePoint integration
benefits.body: Native integration with SharePoint, iManage, OneDrive, Microsoft Teams, and Office 365. Works within your existing document management environment.

tco.heading: 3-year cost comparison — 10 users
tco.note: Adobe Acrobat business/teams pricing as published on adobe.com/au, 2 March 2026, incl. GST. Tungsten Power PDF Advanced one-time purchase price incl. GST.

proofPoints: Formerly Kofax — trusted enterprise PDF software since 1992
Deployed in 70+ countries including Australian government agencies
Perpetual licensing — own it forever, no forced subscriptions
Local Australian support from VRA — 25 years experience

steps.title: Submit your enquiry
steps.text: Tell us about your organisation size, current PDF solution, and deployment requirements.

steps.title: Receive a tailored proposal
steps.text: We confirm the right edition — Standard, Advanced or Business — with volume pricing and procurement options for your sector.

steps.title: Trial and deploy
steps.text: Download a free 15-day trial, validate in your environment, and deploy with local onboarding from our Australian team.

features.title: Professional redaction and legal workflows
features.body: Permanently redact sensitive information, apply Bates numbering and stamping, set document permissions, and manage digital signatures. Built for legal, government, and compliance environments.

features.title: Cloud editing and Microsoft 365 integration
features.body: Power PDF 2025.3 introduces browser-based cloud editing and deep Microsoft Teams, OneDrive and SharePoint integration. Work on PDFs anywhere, on any device, without leaving your existing workflow.

faqs.q: Is Tungsten Power PDF a subscription or a one-time purchase?
faqs.a: Power PDF Standard, Advanced and Mac are perpetual licences — you pay once and own the software permanently with no ongoing fees. Power PDF Business is available as a subscription for organisations requiring centralised management and cloud access.

faqs.q: How much cheaper is Power PDF than Adobe Acrobat?
faqs.a: Adobe Acrobat Pro for teams costs A$419.89 per user per year in Australia. Power PDF Advanced is A$261 per user once — a one-time purchase. For 10 users over 3 years, that's A$2,610 vs A$12,597. Adobe pricing as published on adobe.com/au, 2 March 2026.

faqs.q: What does Power PDF Advanced include over Standard?
faqs.a: Advanced adds SharePoint and iManage integrations, e-signature workflows, batch processing, Bates numbering and stamping, and advanced redaction — plus everything in Standard. It is the recommended edition for legal, government, and enterprise environments.

faqs.q: Is it suitable for Australian government procurement?
faqs.a: Yes — Tungsten Power PDF is deployed in government agencies across Australia and supports standard procurement pathways including panel arrangements and volume licensing. Local support and onboarding is provided by our Australian team.

faqs.q: Does it integrate with SharePoint and Microsoft 365?
faqs.a: Yes — Power PDF Advanced integrates natively with SharePoint, iManage, Microsoft 365, OneDrive, and Teams. It works within your existing document management environment without disruption.
Yes — Power PDF is available as a free 15-day trial with full functionality. Contact us and we will arrange access along with onboarding support from our Australian team.
